data.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

Verified all five acceptance criteria against the repository. Every requirement is met.

---

### AC1 — Scorer registry mapping every scorer name to a pure function ✅

`src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py` (lines 203–224) defines `REGISTRY: dict[str, Scorer]` with 15 entries covering all scorer-name archetypes required by the specs: schema/contract (`emits_valid_review_result`, `emits_valid_findings`, `emits_valid_verdict`, `emits_valid_verification`), recall (`recall_on_seeded_defects`, `recall_on_silent_drop`, `recall_on_incomplete`, `recall_on_gaps_and_conflicts`), no-fire (`no_fire_on_good_cases`, `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope`, `no_false_fail_on_complete`, `no_fire_on_aligned`), grounding (`cites_real_paths`), and discrimination (`discriminates_true_from_false`, `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects`). Each maps to a pure `(dict, dict) -> ScoreResult` function. `test_every_packaged_deterministic_scorer_is_registered` (test line 17–27) programmatically cross-checks all packaged YAML spec scorer names against the registry.

### AC2 — `validate_scorer` rejects unregistered deterministic scorer names ✅

`validate_scorer` (eval.py lines 107–175) accepts an optional `known: frozenset[str]` parameter; when provided and the deterministic scorer name is absent, it appends `"...is not a registered scorer (rebar.llm.eval_scorers.REGISTRY)..."` (lines 127–131). `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` populates `known` from `known_scorer_names()` and threads it through (lines 301–307). The lenient default (`strict=False`) leaves `known=None`, preserving backward compatibility. Confirmed by `test_strict_rejects_unregistered_scorer` (test lines 197–202).

### AC3 — Dataset-case schema defined + validated; scan_spec batch shape supported ✅

`validate_dataset_and_gold` (eval.py lines 200–262) enforces: non-empty `dataset`, unique `id` per case, `expect` drawn from `ALLOWED_EXPECTS` (the union of `finding|fail|pass|high_validity|low_validity`), at least one payload key outside the metadata key set (`id`, `corpus`, `expect`, `criterion`, `kind`, `pair`, `mode`, `note`, `label`), per-axis balance (≥1 fire AND ≥1 pass, or both `high_validity` + `low_validity`), and non-empty `gold_set` with `input`+`label` per entry. The docstring (line 206–207) explicitly names `spec`+`epics` as the scan_spec BATCH unit payload — since neither is in `metadata_keys`, such cases pass the payload check generically. All checks exercised by tests `test_strict_requires_dataset_and_gold`, `test_strict_requires_balance`, and `test_strict_catches_missing_payload_and_dupe_id`.

### AC4 — Unit tests cover registry, each scorer's pass/fail, and new validation ✅

`tests/unit/test_eval_scorers.py` has 24 tests covering: registry completeness + unknown-name dispatch, recall/no-fire pass and fail paths, completion-verdict firing, schema scorers (`emits_valid_review_result`, `emits_valid_verdict` including FAIL⟺findings contract and citation requirement), `cites_real_paths` (resolved vs. downgraded citations), verifier discrimination (`discriminates_true_from_false`, `no_sycophancy_on_real_defects`), validity extraction from labels, all strict-validation paths, and an integration smoke-test that all three plan-review packaged specs pass `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`.

### AC5 — No live model calls; all offline + deterministic ✅

`eval_scorers.py` imports only stdlib (`collections.abc`, `dataclasses`) plus a lazy internal `from rebar.llm.findings import ...` inside scorer bodies — no HTTP, no `inspect_ai`, no model API. `validate_eval_spec`/`validate_scorer`/`validate_dataset_and_gold` are similarly stdlib-only. Tests import only `yaml`, `glob`, and the two rebar modules; no mocking of model calls is needed. The module docstring states "All offline, no model — the scorers are pure functions."

---

**Overall verdict: PASS.** All five acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied by the implementation in the repository.
